standard_description: Represent whole numbers as lengths from 0 on a number line diagram with equally spaced points corresponding to the numbers 0, 1, 2, ..., and represent whole-number sums and differences within 100 on a number line diagram.
subject: Math

Task
One day, Frog and Toad were sitting together on a lily pad. Some lily pads were in a line across the pond.
###IMAGE0###
In the morning, Frog hopped three lily pads away. In the afternoon, he hopped two more away. In the evening, he hopped another two more.
Toad hopped four lily pads away in the morning. He rested in the afternoon and continued three further in the evening. Frog said,
Toad, we ended up at the same place!
Show each of their journeys on a number line, starting at $0$. Use different colors for the morning, afternoon, and evening hops. Write a number sentence that reflects that they ended up at the same place.

metadata.title: Frog and Toad on the number line

c.   What is the distance, in miles, between the park and the market?

Problem 2
Make a number line to correspond with each of your fraction strips by doing the following:
Draw a horizontal line with your straightedge that is a bit longer than one of your fraction strips.
Place a whole fraction strip just above the line you drew.
Make a small tick mark on your line that is even with the left end of your strip. Label the number 0.
Make a small tick mark on your line that is even with the right end of your strip. Label the number 1.
Make small tick marks to show the fractions’ locations. Label the tick marks with their fractions.
Repeat the process to create a number line for each of your fraction strips.

Problem 1
Mr. Ingall, Mrs. Ingall’s husband, spotted a fly on the wall in their house. He wanted to catch it and let it free outside, but he hates bugs. How should he describe to Mrs. Ingall where the fly is on the wall so that she can catch it?
###IMAGE0###
Problem 2
Play a round of Guess Which One.
Sit next to your partner.
Partner A: Mentally choose a card (cut out from
Template: What’s the Point
) without indicating which card you chose. Then, describe the location of the point to your partner.
Partner B: When you have enough information to determine which card your partner chose, guess which one it was.
Switch roles and play another round of Guess Which One.
Use words to describe the location of the point on the grid below.

###IMAGE1###

Ms. Needham knows there are 283 students in Grades 3, 4, and 5 at Fishtank Learning Elementary School. She says that there are about 280 students.

b.   Ms. Needham rounded the number of Grade 3, 4, and 5 students to be able to set up seats in the gym for an assembly with those grades. Do you think Ms. Needham made a good decision about how she rounded the number of students? Why or why not?

Problem 1
A coat costs $92. Mrs. Ingall says the coat is about $90. Mr. Silver says it is about $100. Who is correct, Mrs. Ingall, Mr. Silver, both of them, or neither of them? Explain your answer.
Problem 2
When a number is rounded to the nearest ten, it rounds to the lesser benchmark. When a number is rounded to the nearest hundred, it rounds to the greater benchmark. What might the number be?

tasks.task_title: Frog and Toad on the number line
tasks.task_text:
2.MD Frog and Toad on the
number line
Task
One day, Frog and Toad were sitting together on a lily pad. Some lily pads were in a line
across the pond.
In the morning, Frog hopped three lily pads away. In the afternoon, he hopped two
more away. In the evening, he hopped another two more.
Toad hopped four lily pads away in the morning. He rested in the afternoon and
continued three further in the evening. Frog said,
Toad, we ended up at the same place!
 
Show each of their journeys on a number line, starting at . Use different colors for the
morning, afternoon, and evening hops. Write a number sentence that reflects that they
ended up at the same place.
2.MD Frog and Toad on the number line
